Risk Title: High Defect Rate in Sprint Execution
Risk Description: Consistent pattern of high defect counts (68 actual vs 50 planned) across multiple sprints with medium defect leakage detected. Multiple defects requiring 2-3 retesting cycles indicating incomplete fixes and quality issues.
Potential Impact: Reduced software reliability and increased rework costs

Risk Description: Consistent PR approval delays across all sprints causing cycle time increases. Average PR approval time of 2.5 days impacting development velocity and sprint completion timelines.
Potential Impact: Sprint delays and reduced team productivity

Risk Description: QA costs exceeded budget by ₹25,000 (8.3% overrun) due to higher retesting cycles. Actual spend ₹3,25,000 vs planned ₹3,00,000 indicating scope creep or underestimation.
Potential Impact: Financial loss and reduced profit margins

Risk Description: DevOps costs exceeded budget by ₹20,000 (10% overrun) due to increased production monitoring requirements. Actual spend ₹2,20,000 vs planned ₹2,00,000.
Recurring blocks due to UAT/prod environment access permissions causing delays across multiple sprints. Tasks frequently blocked waiting for access approvals and environment readiness.
Potential Impact: Delivery delays and team idle time

Risk Description: Reopen count increased from planned 5 to actual 12 (140% increase) indicating incomplete fixes and quality control issues. Multiple defects reopened due to incomplete fixes requiring additional retesting cycles.
Infrastructure costs exceeded budget by ₹15,000 (15% overrun) due to cloud usage spike. Actual spend ₹1,15,000 vs planned ₹1,00,000 indicating resource underestimation.
Automation coverage at 75% vs planned 80% (5% shortfall) and overall coverage at 92% vs planned 95% (3% shortfall) indicating testing gaps and potential quality risks.
Potential Impact: Increased manual testing effort and quality risks

Risk Description: Multiple sprints (Sprint 2, 4, 6, 8) completed with 1-day delays indicating consistent scheduling or estimation issues. Pattern of slight delays across 50% of sprints.
Production monitoring workload increased from planned 10% to actual 14% (40% increase) causing DevOps budget overrun and resource strain.
Developer utilization at 82% vs planned 70% (17% overutilization) indicating resource strain and potential burnout risk. Higher workload due to increased fix requirements.
QA utilization at 24% vs planned 20% (20% overutilization) due to increased retesting cycles and defect management workload.
3 production incidents recorded with MTTR of 4.2 hours. While uptime is stable at 99.8%, incidents indicate potential system stability concerns requiring monitoring.
Consistent blocks and delays due to dependency tracking issues including access permissions, approvals, and environment readiness. Priya M's spike tasks frequently blocked or in progress.
Production support blocked by certificate renewal and production log access issues causing operational delays and monitoring gaps.
Client UAT signoff and defect retest queue causing blocks in QA validation and testing progress. Multiple instances of blocked status due to UAT dependencies.
Cutover checklist approval and interface freeze confirmation blocking integration validation and production readiness activities.
Multiple instances of planned leave, sick leave, and flexi holidays requiring backup coverage. While coverage maintained, indicates resource dependency and potential single points of failure.
Weekly health status degraded from Green (Week 1-5) to Amber (Week 6-8) indicating accumulating issues and risks requiring management attention.
Potential Impact: Project health deterioration and stakeholder concern

Risk Description: Code coverage at 88% vs industry standard of 90%+ indicating potential quality gaps and untested code paths.
Defect density at 1.2/KLOC while acceptable, is approaching threshold levels requiring monitoring to prevent quality degradation.
Integration validation tasks frequently facing delays due to environment access and external dependencies. Keerthi L's tasks show recurring dependency-related delays.
Potential Impact: Integration delays and data flow issues

Risk Description: Bala completing hypercare handover and rolling off after Sprint 8 with support transitioned to remaining operations. Indicates knowledge transfer and continuity risk.
While stakeholder satisfaction is high at 4.2/5, the degrading weekly health status and accumulating delays pose risk to continued satisfaction.
Total operational budget overrun of ₹9,000 (4.5% overrun) with ₹2,09,000 actual vs ₹2,00,000 planned. While acceptable, indicates budget control concerns.
Potential Impact: Financial constraints and profit margin impact

Risk Description: Sprint velocity at 42 is high and consistent, but combined with high developer utilization (82%) indicates potential sustainability risk for future sprints.
Retest cycle time at 1.8 days is efficient but with high defect reopen rates (12 vs 5 planned), indicates quality vs speed trade-off concerns.
Recurring production queue, exception alerts, and retry behavior monitoring indicating potential system stability and data flow concerns requiring continuous attention.
Potential Impact: System stability and data integrity risks

Risk Description: Multiple instances of sick leave (Nisha Sprint 1 Day 3, Divya Sprint 4 Day 6, Priya M Sprint 6 Day 4) requiring backup coverage indicating team health and availability concerns.
Multiple flexi holidays (Priya M Sprint 2 Day 5, Nisha Sprint 7 Day 2) requiring backup coverage indicating resource planning and coverage coordination needs.
Q2 2026 forecast shows projected cost of ₹14,50,000 with profit of ₹3,50,000 and ROI of 124%. While positive, requires monitoring to ensure forecast accuracy given Q1 overruns.

Risk Description: Client approval at 98% is high but indicates 2% gap requiring attention to ensure full sign-off and acceptance.
Team morale at 4.5/5 is high but with high utilization rates (82% dev, 24% QA, 14% DevOps) and budget overruns, sustainability of morale requires monitoring.
Potential Impact: Team morale degradation and retention risks

Risk Description: Task status distribution shows 8% Open, 12% In Progress, 5% Blocked indicating 25% of tasks not yet completed, requiring focus to ensure sprint closure.
Sprint 8 focused on hypercare, stabilization, handover, and closure. Multiple tasks still in progress and blocked status indicating potential closure and handover risks.
